title: xyris moraesii
intro: Xyris moraesii, also known as the Brazilian yellow-eyed grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Xyris moraesii typically include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
